516, Lok Nayak Bhawan, New Delhi
Dated the 20th SePtember, 2016
OFFICE MEMORANDUM
Subject Relaxation of Norms for Startups Medium Enterprises in Public
Procurement regarding Prior Experience - Prior Turnover criteria.
The undersigned is directed to refer to this Department O.M. of even number
dated 25th July, 2016, wherein it was clarified that all Central Ministries/ Departments
may relaxcondition of prior turnover and prior experience in public procurement to all
Start-ups [whether Micro & Small Enterprises (MSEs) or otherwiseJ subJect to
meeting of quality and technical specifications in accordance with the relevant
provisions of GFR, 2005.
2. 
A doubt has arisen if it makes optionalfor Central Ministries/ Departments to
relax condition of prior experience bnd prior turnover in public procurement to
Startups. ln this regard, it is again clarified that normally for alt public procurement,
the Central Ministries/ Departments have to ensure that criteria of prior turnover and
prior experlence for all Startups is relaxed subjeci to their meeting of quality and
technicalspeciftcations. 
..
3, 
However, there may be circumstances (like procurement of items related to
public safety, health, critical securityloperations and equipments, etc.) where
procuring entities may prefer the vendors to have prior experience rather than
giving orders to new entities. For such procurements, wherever adequate
justification exists, the procuring entities may not relax the criteria of prior
experience/ tumover for the $tartups.
4. 
This issues with the approval of Finance Secretary. 
Yrty
( Vinayaft T. Likhar )
Under Secretary (PPD)

0FFICE MFItrlp RA]l.p Ul-vI
Subject- Relaxation of Norms for Startups Medium Enterprises in Public
Procurement regarding Prior Experience - Prior Turnover criteria.
The Government of lndia has announced 'startup lndia' initiative for
creating a conducive ecosystem for the growth of Startups in lndia. The Startups
are defined lri Annexure-A of the "Action Plan for Etartups lndia". The sarne is
evailable on the website of Department of lndustrial Policy and Promotion
(DIPP), Mlnistry of Commerce & lndustry.
2. 
Ministry of"Micro, $mall & Medium Enterprises (MSMES) vide Policy
Circular No.1(2)(1y2016-MA dated 10th March,2016 has clarified that all Central
Ministries / Departments / Central Public $ector Under{akings (CPSUS) rnay
relax condition of prior turnover and prior experience wlth respect of Mioro &
Small Enterprises (MSEs) in gll public procurements subject to meeting of quality
and teclinical specifi cations.
3. 
As per Rule 160(iXa) of GFR, 2005, there is already a provision that the
bidding document ahould contain criteria for eligibllity and qualification to be met
by the bidders such as minimum level of experience, pasi performance, technical
capablllty, rnanufacturing facilities and financial position etc, ln view of above, it
is further clarified that all Central Ministries I Departments may relax condition of
prior turnwer and prior experience in public procurernent to all Startups (whether
MSEs or otherwise) subject to meeting of quality and technical speciflcations in
acCtrdance with the relevant provisions of GFR, 2005.

To
All Central M in istries/Departments/C PSU s/All Concerned
Subject Relaxation of Norms for $tartups and Micro & Small Enterprises ln
Publlc Procurement on Prlor Experience - Prlor Turnover crlterla,
(1) The Government of lndia has notified Public Procurement Policy for Micro and
Small Enterprises (MSEs) Order 2012 with effect from 1't April,2012 and 200/o
procurement from Micro & Small Enterprises of the total procurement by Central
Mlnistries/Departments/CPSUs has become mandatory with effect from lsrApril,
2015.
(2) The Government of lndia has announced 'startup lndia' initiative for creating a
conducive environment for Starlups in lndia.
(3) The Startups are normally Micro and Small Enterprises which may not have a
track record. These will have technical capability to deliver the goods and
services as per prescribed technical& quality specifications, and may not be able
to meel the quaiification criterion relating to prior experience-prior turnover.
(4) ln exercise of Para 16 of Public Procurement Policy for Micro and Small
Enterprises Order 2012, it is clarified that allCentral Ministries/Departments/
Central Public Sector Undertakings may relax condition of prior turnover and
prior experience with respect to Micro and Small Enterprises in all public
procurements subject to meeting of quality and technical specifications.
(5) This issues with the approval of Union Minister of Micro, Small and Medium
Enterprises
